---
title: "Labs"
---

This section starts with a guided `lalonde` walkthrough that mirrors the main report, then collects the follow-on worked examples that extend the same workflow to other datasets.

For a short concept-first overview before these exercises, start with [Why We Do Matching](../slides/why_matching.qmd) and [Why Matching Presenter Script](../slides/why_matching_script.qmd).

Use these pages as part of the same design-first workflow:

- start with the `lalonde` walkthrough if you want the lab version of the main report itself
- move next to the benchmarked policy-style examples if you want the closest dataset extensions to the main report
- treat each lab as a self-contained notebook with its own assumptions, diagnostics, and tradeoffs

## Available Labs

| Lab | Dataset(s) | Current status | Link |
|---|---|---|---|
| LaLonde Main Report Lab | `MatchIt::lalonde` | Guided worksheet aligned to the main report | [Open](lalonde_main_report_lab.qmd) · [Main report](../articles/matching_methods_report.qmd) · [Full code](../articles/matching_methods_report_full_code.qmd) · [Evaluated code](lalonde_main_report_lab_evaluated_code.qmd) · [QA log](../articles/matching_methods_report_qa_log.qmd) |
| Black Politicians Lab | `causaldata::black_politicians` | Training aid | [Open](black_politicians_lab.qmd) · [Full code](black_politicians_lab_full_code.qmd) · [Evaluated code](black_politicians_lab_evaluated_code.qmd) · [QA log](black_politicians_lab_qa_log.qmd) |
| HISP IE in Practice Lab | `evaluation.dta` converted to `evaluation.csv` from the HISP case-study bundle | Stata-to-R replication lab | [Open](hisp_ie_practice_lab.qmd) · [Full code](hisp_ie_practice_lab_full_code.qmd) · [Evaluated code](hisp_ie_practice_lab_evaluated_code.qmd) · [QA log](hisp_ie_practice_lab_qa_log.qmd) |
| NSW + CPS Benchmark Lab | `causaldata::nsw_mixtape`, `causaldata::cps_mixtape` | Benchmark lab | [Open](nsw_cps_benchmark_lab.qmd) · [Full code](nsw_cps_benchmark_lab_full_code.qmd) · [Evaluated code](nsw_cps_benchmark_lab_evaluated_code.qmd) · [QA log](nsw_cps_benchmark_lab_qa_log.qmd) |

## Recommended Sequence

1. Use the concept deck and presenter script first if you want the shortest delivery version of the matching logic.
2. Start with the LaLonde main report lab if you want the worksheet version of the core `ATT` comparison.
3. Move to the Black Politicians lab for the first dataset extension with richer covariates.
4. Use the NSW + CPS benchmark lab when you want to compare observational designs against an experimental reference.
5. Use the HISP IE in Practice lab when you want a Stata-to-R replication that walks through weaker and stronger impact-evaluation designs in the order used in the book case study.

## Working Rule

Keep the same standard across all labs: define the estimand first, inspect imbalance before adjustment, and compare balance against retention after design changes.
